title: papaver dubium subsp. collinum
intro: Papaver dubium subsp. collinum, also known as the lesser meadow poppy or colline poppy, is a subspecies of the Papaver dubium plant, a flowering plant in the poppy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its alkaloids, including papaverine and thebaine. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved sleep quality due to its sedative properties. Herbal preparations of Papaver dubium subsp. collinum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
